mainText:
Good morning. Here are your headlines for Sunday 24 May 2026.

AI models and products. Subquadratic's SubQ 1M-Preview launched May 5 with 29 million seed funding claiming a non-transformer architecture.

UK personal finance. Trading 212 offers 4.62 percent easy access ISA for new money.

Israeli politics. 110 of 120 lawmakers voted to advance a dissolution bill on 20 May.

UK politics. Labour lost more than 1,100 council seats while Reform UK gained more than 1,400 in May 7 elections.

Sleep and wearables. Google launches Fitbit Air at $99.99 with AI Coach and integrated Gemini features.

B2B SaaS and go-to-market. AI companies command 42 percent premium at seed and 70 percent at Series A, though investor focus is shifting toward proven ROI over hype.

Today's question: With sales cycles compressing to 19 weeks and hybrid pricing now the norm, are you positioning your GTM advisory around helping Israeli tech founders navigate the UK market faster by bundling consumption-based pricing with proof-of-concept infrastructure?
fullText:
Good morning. Here is your daily brief for Sunday 24 May 2026.

🤖 AI models and products
- GPT-5.5 Instant replaced GPT-5.3 as default ChatGPT model on May 5, scoring 81.2 percent on AIME 2025 math versus 65.4 for its predecessor.
- ZAYA1-8B from Zyphra is open-source Apache 2.0 MoE with 8 billion total parameters, roughly 760 million active per token, trained end-to-end on AMD Instinct hardware and available free on Hugging Face.
- xAI's Grok 4.3 launched May 6 at 1.25 and 2.50 per million tokens with 1 million context window and vision support, priced below every other frontier model except DeepSeek.

💷 UK personal finance
- Best one-year fixed ISA rate reached 4.70 percent AER as of 22 May 2026, with two-year fixed rates hitting 4.71 percent AER, making fixed products competitive for longer-term savers.
- Trading 212 leads easy access accounts at 4.62 percent while Moneybox offers 4.33 percent for transfers and Bank of Ireland UK 4.21 percent for traditional bank appeal.
- Major mortgage lenders including Nationwide, HSBC, Halifax and Santander have been cutting mortgage rates in May 2026, though the Bank of England held interest rates at 3.75 percent on 30 April and pointed to likelihood of higher rates and possibility of "forceful" rises.

🇮🇱 Israeli politics
- Netanyahu's fractious right-wing coalition faces possible collapse amid mounting pressure from ultra-Orthodox parties.
- Netanyahu told ultra-Orthodox leaders he would not advance military exemption legislation, causing Degel HaTorah faction to declare "We have no trust in Netanyahu anymore" and call to dissolve parliament.
- Bennett and Lapid seek to broaden their opposition coalition by inviting Gadi Eisenkot, former IDF chief of staff and leader of Yashar Party, to join their new party.

🇬🇧 UK politics
- Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigned from Cabinet on May 14, and Labour MP Josh Simons resigned to allow Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham to return to parliament and stand for the Labour leadership.
- Labour lost control of Lambeth and Lewisham councils to the Green Party, with Green Party leader Zack Polanski saying Starmer needs to go.
- Reform took over Havering, its first London local authority, plus Essex county council and Sunderland, with Starmer describing his government as a 10-year project of renewal despite facing pressure to resign.

😴 Sleep and wearables
- Fitbit Air is available for pre-order at $99, with wider shipping starting May 26, 2026. The device weighs just 12 grams, making it lighter than the Whoop.
- Oura Ring costs $349 with a $5.99 monthly subscription. Whoop uses a subscription-only model at $30 per month with fourteen-day battery life and refined recovery algorithms.
- Muse S Athena is the only consumer sleep wearable combining EEG and fNIRS for 88-96 percent PSG-validated sleep stage accuracy, with real-time sleep intervention and no subscription required.

📈 B2B SaaS and go-to-market
- Sales cycle length compressed from roughly 25 weeks in 2025 to about 19 weeks in 2026, rewarding SaaS teams that streamline evaluation processes with strong proof-of-concept infrastructure.
- Forty-eight percent of SaaS companies now report hybrid pricing as their primary model, with consumption-based and usage-based structures gaining ground, driven by enterprise buyers resisting fixed multi-year seat commitments.
- AI SDR agents execute thousands of personalized outreach messages daily at 500 dollars per month versus 60 thousand to 90 thousand annually for human SDRs, with 86 percent of startup owners reporting positive outcomes from AI in go-to-market strategies.

Today's question: With sales cycles compressing to 19 weeks and hybrid pricing now the norm, are you positioning your GTM advisory around helping Israeli tech founders navigate the UK market faster by bundling consumption-based pricing with proof-of-concept infrastructure?
